blockingIssues.description: Lineup-weighted offense metrics are built from season-level FanGraphs batting snapshots with no as-of date scoping, so 7/14/30/60 historical lineup features can leak future player performance and collapse to a non-rolling value.

Opener detection trusts `lineup.is_opener` whenever a lineup object exists, which suppresses pitcher-history fallback for real opener/bullpen games and can skip the required team-composite pitching path.

Defense histories are synthesized from season-level fielding/framing snapshots by stamping one prior-day row, which leaks future season totals into historical dates and prevents real 30/60-game rolling DRS/OAA/framing windows.

The bullpen inherited-runner parser does not recognize `Pitching_*` team-log columns produced by `fetch_team_game_logs(log_type="pitching")`, so live IR% lookups drop all rows and silently fill `*_bullpen_ir_pct_30g` with zeros.

When odds ingestion backfills a missing `games` row, it derives the venue from the home team's default stadium instead of event metadata, so neutral-site ABS exception games can still persist with `is_abs_active = True`.
The weather adjustment module uses a wind-direction sign convention opposite to the existing weather client, so real fetched weather can invert wind impact and distort `weather_composite`.

appliedUpdates.description: Added an `init_windows` command that creates the venv if needed, installs dependencies through `.venv\Scripts\python.exe`, creates data directories, warns about missing `.env`, and initializes SQLite without relying on Git Bash activation behavior.

Added `.factory/library/feature-engineering.md` documenting season-snapshot vs as-of data-source constraints, pitching team-log column naming, canonical weather wind semantics, the SQLite games-table baseline source, and the current odds-backfill venue quirk.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add Windows-specific startup and validation guidance that points feature workers to `.factory/services.yaml` Windows commands and direct `.venv\Scripts\python.exe` invocation instead of relying on `.factory/init.sh` under Git Bash.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: t11-defense-features and t14-weather-engine both reported justified deviations because the current feature-worker procedure omits the Windows shell caveat already captured elsewhere in shared state.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: For baseline-style feature work, explicitly note that historical final/F5 runs can come from the SQLite `games` table before workers reach for pybaseball team-game-log scraping.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: t15-pythagorean-log5 passed, but its reviewer found the worker spent time exploring pybaseball sources before discovering the canonical `games.final_*` and `games.f5_*` columns already satisfied the need.

rejectedObservations.observation: The repeated Windows services/init observation from t13-park-abs-adjustments duplicated the same startup gap already captured from t9-offensive-features.
The repeated feature-worker Windows caveat from t14-weather-engine duplicated the same skill-gap observation already captured from t11-defense-features.
